message:
test(fcp-core): metamorphic relation tests for zone-capability derivations + 3 new beads

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/metamorphic.rs (253 lines) — a targeted
metamorphic-relation suite for the zone-capability APIs exported by
fcp-core. Pins algebraic properties of the CapabilityToken,
CapabilityVerifier, and RevocationRegistry surfaces so future
refactors cannot quietly weaken the security model.

## Scope

Tests exercise the real fcp-core exports (not mocks, not internal
helpers) — CapabilityId, CapabilityToken, CapabilityVerifier,
RevocationObject, RevocationRegistry, ZoneBound, ZoneId, plus
supporting types (ObjectHeader, ObjectId, OperationId, Provenance,
SealValidation). Metamorphic relations chosen for their algebraic
strength: if any relation fails, some invariant the security
argument depends on has regressed.

## Fixtures

`fixed_validity_window()` pins the test time range to
2026-04-22T00:00:00Z..01:00:00Z — deterministic, no clock drift,
no sampling of Utc::now(). Token validity is always "1 hour
starting at the fixed timestamp" so verify-time predicates always
have the same truth value across runs.

`wildcard_constraints_cbor()` builds the "allow everything"
CapabilityConstraints and serializes to CBOR once. Re-used by every
test that doesn't specifically exercise resource_allow filtering —
keeps the test surface focused on the relation under test rather
than the constraints-encoding path.

## Properties pinned (typical shape)

Most relations follow the pattern:
  1. Build a CapabilityToken with fixture (zone, caps, validity).
  2. Apply an operation expected to preserve a property (e.g.
     derive a sub-zone-bound token, revoke a sibling cap, age the
     clock by N seconds).
  3. Assert the property still holds on the mutated token.

The specific relations covered (one per top-level test fn in the
file) are:
  - Token verification is stable under token re-encoding (CBOR
    round-trip preserves verify result).
  - Revocation is monotonic — once revoked, always revoked for
    subsequent verifier queries under the same registry.
  - ZoneBound narrowing only rejects, never admits — a token valid
    under a broader zone bound must verify at least as strictly
    under a narrower one.
  - (plus several more; full list inside the file via `#[test]`
    names)

## 3 new beads

File three placeholder beads that surfaced during the metamorphic
suite construction — work that the author noticed but didn't scope
into this commit:

  - flywheel_connectors-8n0rm (open, no body yet — scaffolding for
    the next author to fill in).
  - flywheel_connectors-jkcka (open).
  - flywheel_connectors-lse4w (open).

Each is a skeleton JSONL entry so the tracker graph has a place to
hang follow-up context without losing the reference. Bodies get
filled in the next beads-curation pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
